rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WINSTON R. LOUIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/31/23. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] The report includes a statement that an "Inventory and Appraisal part 1" has been filed; the only I&A filed on 6/6/22 was a "Final" I&A. The report should include a petition seeking additional time for administration if the estate is not ready to close. Appearance will be required on 1/31/23 even if no proposed order is submitted, if the Corrected Final I&A and an amendment to this report (petition) are not filed in time to (1) give notice of the amendment to the sole heir and (2) deliver courtesy copies of the amendment and the Corrected I&A to the examiner by 1/20/23.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL DORIA PALACIOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/31/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each beneficiary, not that assets will still be sold after final distribution is ordered. The lots of Alphabet stock were not how assets were reported in either I&A. Either the Amended I&A must be amended again to show the shares as set forth in the amendment filed on 11/14/22 or the report must be amended (1) to report to the Court that x number of shares of stock were sold, leaving x number of shares on hand + an amount of cash as of a date certain and (2) to propose distribution of the remaining shares of stock and remaining cash from the liquidated shares, after deducting fees and costs requested, equally to each heir. (The value of securities is never ordered to be distributed; only the number of shares.) How would there be $407.13 remaining in the attorney's IOLTA account from the $1,000 the administrator deposited? (Paragraph 5.b. is confusing.) A SECOND AMENDMENT to the petition, signed and verified by the administrator, must be filed to address these issues. Notice must be given of the second amendment after it is e-filed, or the heirs could waive notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/31/23. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(c)(2).] A paralegal billed 2.0 hours to attend a hearing on 7/9/21? Bond is currently $10,000, not $71,000? Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions To Enforce The No Contest Clause Against Matthew A. Guerra, To Ascertain Beneficiaries Of The Theodore P. Glaza Trust, And To Compel Trust Information
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/14/23. Still no proposed order and no courtesy copy of a supplement to the petition, setting forth ALL vested or contingent beneficiaries of the trust. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Deadline for providing these was 12/9/22. New deadline for submitting the proposed order and courtesy copy of a supplement to the petition (and any further filings in support of the petition or objections) must be received by the assigned examiner by 2/3/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD J. GIETZEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The amended petition, while file stamped 10/28/22, when it was uploaded, was not actually e-filed until 10/31/22, when it first became available for viewing. Notice is defective if mailed before the actual filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice. Furthermore, the proof of service also filed on 10/28/22 does not show that an AMENDED petition was filed. The caption of the amended petition is still incomplete if full IAEA authority is being requested. The caption of the amended petition must be supplemented. This Court requires an ex parte petition to be filed to dispense with notice to a person whose whereabouts are unknown. (Exhibits attached to a declaration setting forth efforts to locate the person should have any personal information redacted.) Only the cover page of the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number was received by the examiner. Courtesy copies of the supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the proposed executor, completing the caption of the amended petition, and of the proof of service of the amended petition must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/20/23 to avoid another continuance. If an ex parte petition has not been filed and granted in the meantime to waive notice to the cousin, whose whereabouts are unknown, the hearing may need to be continued further. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARRY KENNARD WILSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Objection or Response not on file by 11/28/2022. The the hearing is for the sole purpose of admitting the will. CRC 7.801. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA T. GUERRERO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The proposed personal representative was missing from item 8 of the petition; however, because JC form DE-147 shows her address, this defect is waived. The name of the newspaper is also missing from item 1 of the petition, but notice appears to have been published properly. Status of closing the estate is set for 1/8/2024. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 1/8/24. Appearance will be required on 1/8/24 if no petition has been filed. No SASE was provided; copies of signed orders cannot be returned to Counsel.
